key_insight: TREK expands a student model’s reasoning support with verified teacher-generated trajectories before reinforcing them on-policy, helping GRPO escape hard-prompt failure modes.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical training recipe for improving reasoning and agent performance using only verified outputs, with support for black-box teachers or even self-context generation.
method: Find prompts where the student has very low pass rate.
Query a proposal source and keep verified candidate solutions ranked by student likelihood.
Run a short forward-KL distillation phase, then resume standard on-policy GRPO refinement.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, TREK improves Qwen3 on AIME 2024/2025 across tested scales, with Qwen3-8B reaching 40.3 on AIME 2025 and 51.1 on AIME 2024 (avg@16), and also boosts ALFWorld and ScienceWorld success rates.
watch_item: Performance depends on obtaining verified proposals and enough teacher support to expand the student’s support; without good proposal quality, the exploration phase may be less effective.
paper_title: TREK: Distill to Explore, Reinforce to Refine
